headline: Wall Street Lunch: Quantinuum Soars 12% On Nasdaq Debut Following $1.7B IPO
summary:
Quantinuum (QNT) debuted on the Nasdaq at $68 a share, up 12% from its IPO price.
The quantum computing company, which is majority owned by Honeywell (HON), sold 28M shares at $60 each.

headline: SBUX Down 9% in a Month: Is This the Right Time to Buy the Stock?
summary: Starbucks' shares fall 9% in a month, but improving traffic, rewards growth, innovation and margin recovery efforts may support a turnaround.

headline: Software engineer quit a $250,000 job to open a matcha cafe in Manhattan: 'I wanted purpose and meaning'
summary: Michelle Yeung walked away from a lucrative software engineering career to open a matcha cafe on Manhattan's Lower East Side.

raw.headline: Software engineer quit a $250,000 job to open a matcha cafe in Manhattan: 'I wanted purpose and meaning'

headline: I quit my $250K/year tech job–now I make $33K/year selling matcha
summary: Michelle Yeung, 29, left a $250,000 a year software engineering job in New York to open Matcha House, a cafe on Manhattan’s Lower East Side. In 2026, she expects to pay herself about $33,000, an 87% pay cut, but says she’s happy with her decision.

headline: Cardiologist: 5 things I never do before 9 a.m.—after 20 years of treating heart attacks
summary: Dr. Sanjay Bhojraj, a cardiologist who has treated heart disease for over 20 years, shares the morning habits he avoids to protect cardiovascular health and reduce unnecessary stress on the body. Instead, his routine centers on protein, fiber, hydration and stress management.
